```json
{
  "module": "database-api",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 25,
    "domain_depth": 52,
    "data_richness": 38,
    "interaction_quality": 35,
    "ai_integration": 20,
    "mobile_first": 15,
    "performance": 48,
    "typescript_quality": 62,
    "error_handling": 55,
    "realtime": 42,
    "accessibility": 10,
    "security": 58,
    "differentiation": 30,
    "enterprise_readiness": 45
  },
  "overall_score": 38,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "database-api-C7-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "data_richness",
      "title": "No seed data SQL file exists — all pages show empty states instead of realistic construction data",
      "file": "supabase/seed.sql",
      "description": "The supabase/seed/ directory is empty per FOUNDER PRIORITIES P3. Without seed data, every list page renders empty states, making the app feel unfinished. GCs evaluating the product see nothing. This is a P0 blocker for demos.",
      "prompt": "Create the file supabase/seed.sql with comprehensive construction seed data. This is a NEW file creation. Include: 1 organization ('Turner Construction') with id 'org-00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', 1 project ('Riverside Commercial Tower') with id 'aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa' and organization_id referencing Turner, contract_value 48500000, status 'active', start_date '2025-09-01', target_completion '2027-03-15'. Insert 8 project_members rows matching the test user UUIDs from VERIFICATION_TESTS.md (11111111... through 88888888...) with roles project_manager, superintendent, project_engineer, safety_manager, field_engineer, subcontractor, owner_rep, viewer. Insert 25 RFIs across statuses (12 open, 5 under_review, 4 answered, 4 closed) with realistic construction titles like 'Structural steel connection detail at grid line F7', 'HVAC duct routing conflict above ceiling grid Level 3', priority mix, due_dates spanning past and future. Insert 15 submittals (3 draft, 4 submitted, 3 approved, 2 approved_as_noted, 2 rejected, 1 resubmit) with CSI spec sections like '03 30 00', '09 29 00'. Insert 50 punch_list_items across trades (electrical, drywall, painting, plumbing, HVAC, carpentry) with locations like '3rd Floor Room 302', statuses (20 open, 15 in_progress, 10 completed, 5 verified). Insert 30 daily_logs for the last 30 calendar days with weather descriptions, temperature ranges 45-78F, crew_count 35-85, realistic work summaries. Insert 12 budget_items by CSI division (03 Concrete, 05 Metals, 09 Finishes, 22 Plumbing, 23 HVAC, 26 Electrical, etc.) with original_amount summing to 48500000, committed and spent_to_date at realistic percentages. Insert 5 change_orders (2 approved totaling $385,000, 2 pending totaling $142,000, 1 rejected). Insert 8 schedule_phases with baseline dates, percent_complete, and is_critical_path flags. Insert 10 meetings (5 completed OAC meetings, 3 upcoming coordination meetings, 2 safety meetings). All UUIDs should use gen_random_uuid() except the deterministic test user and project IDs. Use integer cents for all monetary values (multiply dollar amounts by 100). Every INSERT must reference existing foreign keys correctly. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "database-api-C7-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "enterprise_readiness",
      "title": "No notification_queue table or dispatch edge function — email notifications cannot fire",
      "file": "supabase/migrations/20260402200000_notification_queue.sql",
      "description": "PRODUCTION_ROADMAP P0-5 requires email notifications. The notification_queue table does not exist. Without it, RFI assignments, overdue alerts, and daily log reminders cannot be queued or sent. This is a dealbreaker for GC adoption.",
      "prompt": "Create the file supabase/migrations/20260402200000_notification_queue.sql with the following migration. BEFORE: No notification_queue table exists. AFTER: A fully RLS-protected notification_queue table exists for async email dispatch. SQL contents: CREATE TABLE notification_queue (id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(), project_id uuid not null references projects(id) on delete cascade, recipient_user_id uuid not null references auth.users(id), recipient_email text not null, template_name text not null, template_data jsonb not null default '{}', entity_type text, entity_id uuid, status text not null default 'pending' check (status in ('pending', 'processing', 'sent', 'failed', 'skipped')), attempts integer not null default 0, max_attempts integer not null default 3, sent_at timestamptz, error text, created_at timestamptz not null default now(), updated_at timestamptz not null default now()); CREATE INDEX idx_notification_queue_status ON notification_queue(status, created_at) WHERE status = 'pending'; CREATE INDEX idx_notification_queue_project ON notification_queue(project_id); CREATE INDEX idx_notification_queue_recipient ON notification_queue(recipient_user_id); ALTER TABLE notification_queue ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; CREATE POLICY 'Project members can view own notifications' ON notification_queue FOR SELECT USING (recipient_user_id = auth.uid()); CREATE POLICY 'System can insert notifications' ON notification_queue FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (project_id IN (SELECT pm.project_id FROM project_members pm WHERE pm.user_id = auth.uid())); Also create a notification_preferences table: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notification_preferences (id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid not null references auth.users(id) on delete cascade unique, rfi_assigned boolean not null default true, rfi_overdue boolean not null default true, submittal_status_change boolean not null default true, change_order_pending boolean not null default true, daily_log_reminder boolean not null default true, punch_item_assigned boolean not null default true, meeting_scheduled boolean not null default true, digest_enabled boolean not null default true, digest_time time not null default '19:00:00', created_at timestamptz default now(), updated_at timestamptz default now()); ALTER TABLE notification_preferences ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; CREATE POLICY 'Users manage own preferences' ON notification_preferences FOR ALL USING (user_id = auth.uid()); CREATE TRIGGER set_updated_at BEFORE UPDATE ON notification_queue FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at(); CREATE TRIGGER set_updated_at_prefs BEFORE UPDATE ON notification_preferences FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at(); After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "database-api-C7-003",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "performance",
      "title": "Activity feed batchFetchEntityLabels fires N parallel queries instead of using a single batched approach for all entity types",
      "file": "src/api/endpoints/activity.ts",
      "description": "batchFetchEntityLabels only handles 'rfi' and 'submittal' entity types. Punch list items, daily logs, change orders, drawings, and meetings are never resolved, causing missing labels in the activity feed. Additionally, the function creates separate Promise.all entries but does not handle all entity types from the feed.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/endpoints/activity.ts, in the batchFetchEntityLabels function: BEFORE: Only 'rfi' and 'submittal' entity types are resolved. All other entity types (punch_list_item, daily_log, change_order, drawing, meeting, budget_item) return no label, causing the activity feed to show raw IDs or 'Unknown' for those items. AFTER: Add resolution blocks for the remaining entity types. After the existing submittal block, add: if (grouped['punch_list_item']?.length) { fetches.push(supabase.from('punch_list_items').select('id, title').eq('project_id', projectId).in('id', grouped['punch_list_item']).then(({ data }) => { for (const row of data ?? []) { const label = row.title; labelMap.set(row.id, label); setCachedEntityLabel(`${projectId}:punch_list_item:${row.id}`, label); } }).catch((err: unknown) => { console.warn('[ActivityFeed] punch_list_item label fetch failed:', err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)); })); } Add similar blocks for: 'change_order' querying change_orders table selecting 'id, title, co_number' with label format 'CO-{co_number} {title}'; 'daily_log' querying daily_logs table selecting 'id, log_date' with label format 'Daily Log {log_date}'; 'drawing' querying drawings table selecting 'id, title, sheet_number' with label format '{sheet_number} {title}'; 'meeting' querying meetings table selecting 'id, title, date' with label format '{title} ({date})'. Each block follows the exact same pattern as the existing rfi and submittal blocks with try/catch via .catch(). After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "database-api-C7-004",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "Budget endpoint getCostData does not exist as a standalone function — pay app and financial calculations have no centralized cost data fetcher",
      "file": "src/api/endpoints/budget.ts",
      "description": "The projects.ts endpoint imports getCostData from './budget' but no such exported function is visible in the provided code. Financial calculations for projected final cost, committed cost, and variance need a single authoritative data source. Without it, budget metrics on the dashboard may show zeros or errors.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/endpoints/budget.ts, add a new exported function getCostData at the end of the file. BEFORE: No getCostData function exists (or it returns incomplete data). AFTER: A comprehensive getCostData function that returns all financial metrics needed by the dashboard, pay apps, and change order impact analysis. Add this function: export async function getCostData(projectId: string): Promise<{ originalBudget: number; approvedChanges: number; revisedBudget: number; committedCost: number; actualCost: number; projectedFinalCost: number; varianceDollars: number; variancePercent: number; contingencyOriginal: number; contingencyUsed: number; contingencyRemaining: number; lineItems: Array<{ id: string; csiCode: string; description: string; originalAmount: number; revisedAmount: number; committed: number; actual: number; projected: number; variance: number }>; }> { await assertProjectAccess(projectId); const [budgetResult, coResult] = await Promise.all([supabase.from('budget_items').select('*').eq('project_id', projectId).order('csi_code'), supabase.from('change_orders').select('id, cost_impact, status').eq('project_id', projectId)]); if (budgetResult.error) throw transformSupabaseError(budgetResult.error); const items = budgetResult.data ?? []; const changeOrders = coResult.data ?? []; const approvedChanges = changeOrders.filter((co: any) => co.status === 'approved').reduce((sum: number, co: any) => sum + (co.cost_impact || 0), 0); const originalBudget = items.reduce((sum: number, i: any) => sum + (i.original_amount || 0), 0); const revisedBudget = originalBudget + approvedChanges; const committedCost = items.reduce((sum: number, i: any) => sum + (i.committed || 0), 0); const actualCost = items.reduce((sum: number, i: any) => sum + (i.spent_to_date || 0), 0); const projectedFinalCost = items.reduce((sum: number, i: any) => sum + (i.forecast_final_cost || i.original_amount || 0), 0); const varianceDollars = revisedBudget - projectedFinalCost; const variancePercent = revisedBudget > 0 ? (varianceDollars / revisedBudget) * 100 : 0; const contingencyItem = items.find((i: any) => (i.csi_code || '').startsWith('01')); const contingencyOriginal = contingencyItem?.original_amount || 0; const contingencyUsed = contingencyItem?.spent_to_date || 0; const contingencyRemaining = contingencyOriginal - contingencyUsed; const lineItems = items.map((i: any) => ({ id: i.id, csiCode: i.csi_code || '', description: i.description || '', originalAmount: i.original_amount || 0, revisedAmount: (i.original_amount || 0), committed: i.committed || 0, actual: i.spent_to_date || 0, projected: i.forecast_final_cost || i.original_amount || 0, variance: (i.original_amount || 0) - (i.forecast_final_cost || i.original_amount || 0) })); return { originalBudget, approvedChanges, revisedBudget, committedCost, actualCost, projectedFinalCost, varianceDollars, variancePercent, contingencyOriginal, contingencyUsed, contingencyRemaining, lineItems }; } Make sure to import assertProjectAccess from '../middleware/projectScope' and transformSupabaseError from the appropriate location if not already imported. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "database-api-C7-005",
      "severity": "high",
      "dimension": "realtime",
      "title": "Field endpoint daily log queries lack real-time subscription setup and optimistic update support",
      "file": "src/api/endpoints/field.ts",
      "description": "The field.ts endpoint provides CRUD for daily logs but no real-time subscription channel setup. Per the module spec, daily_logs must auto-update when other users change data. The PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires that daily log changes from another user reflect within 2 seconds. Currently, the hook pattern in field.ts has no subscription code.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/endpoints/field.ts, add a new exported function subscribeToDailyLogChanges at the end of the file. BEFORE: No real-time subscription function exists for daily logs. Other users' changes to daily logs are invisible until manual page refresh. AFTER: A subscription function that returns an unsubscribe callback, following the exact pattern from CODEBASE_PATTERNS.md. Add: export function subscribeToDailyLogChanges(projectId: string, onInsert: (log: DailyLogRow) => void, onUpdate: (log: DailyLogRow) => void, onDelete: (oldLog: { id: string }) => void): () => void { const channel = supabase.channel(`daily_logs_${projectId}`).on('postgres_changes', { event: 'INSERT', schema: 'public', table: 'daily_logs', filter: `project_id=eq.${projectId}` }, (payload) => { onInsert(payload.new as DailyLogRow); }).on('postgres_changes', { event: 'UPDATE', schema: 'public', table: 'daily_logs', filter: `project_id=eq.${projectId}` }, (payload) => { onUpdate(payload.new as DailyLogRow); }).on('postgres_changes', { event: 'DELETE', schema: 'public', table: 'daily_logs', filter: `project_id=eq.${projectId}` }, (payload) => { onDelete({ id: (payload.old as { id: string }).id }); }).subscribe(); return () => { supabase.removeChannel(channel); }; } Also add a similar function subscribeToPunchListChanges for punch_list_items with the same pattern, accepting onInsert, onUpdate, onDelete callbacks for PunchItemRow. Both functions must import supabase from '../client'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [
    {
      "title": "Intelligent Query Cost Monitor",
      "description": "Add a development-mode query performance overlay that tracks every Supabase query, measures round-trip time, flags N+1 patterns, and suggests index improvements. Show a small floating badge in dev mode: '23 queries, 1.2s total, 2 warnings'. Click to expand a waterfall chart of all queries on the current page. This would be unique in construction PM — no competitor provides database observability to the developer."
    }
  ],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's API layer is REST-based with documented endpoints but no real-time subscriptions — updates require polling. Fieldwire uses WebSockets for punch list updates but has no financial data layer. SiteSync's Supabase real-time subscriptions and RLS-based security are architecturally superior, but the lack of seed data and incomplete entity resolution in the activity feed makes the advantage invisible to evaluators. The notification queue gap is the most critical competitive weakness — Procore sends emails within seconds of RFI assignment, while SiteSync currently sends nothing.",
  "summary": "The Database & API Layer has solid architectural foundations — typed Supabase client, project-scoped middleware, error transformation, and edit locking. TypeScript quality is the strongest dimension at 62. However, critical gaps block production readiness: no seed data means every demo shows empty states (P0), no notification queue means no email alerts (P0 dealbreaker per PRODUCTION_ROADMAP), the activity feed only resolves 2 of 8 entity types leaving most feed items unlabeled, the budget endpoint lacks a centralized getCostData function needed by dashboard and pay apps, and daily log real-time subscriptions are missing despite being required by PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA. The five prompts target: (1) comprehensive seed data creation, (2) notification_queue migration with RLS, (3) activity feed entity label resolution for all types, (4) centralized budget cost data fetcher, and (5) real-time subscription functions for daily logs and punch lists. These changes will move the score from 38 to an estimated 55+ by filling the most impactful data richness, enterprise readiness, and real-time gaps."
}
```
